name: Microcontroller Mondays
description:
Arduinos, Propellers, Raspberry Pi and more - It's Monday night microcontroller madness!

This is a study group where we get together, share, experiment, and work on projects while helping each other learn about various microcontrollers.

Format of this group will be:

If you are new, one of the event hosts will introduce you to everyone and give you a brief tour of our lab and pod studio.

Members are encouraged to show us a project you're working on or talk about a topic you're learning

Project/work time (opening until close): Brainstorm, collaborate, experiment, and hack away!

CDL Lab Resources: Available for lab use- We have numerous Arduino shields and various displays to experiment with, Arduinos, Raspberry Pi, 3D-printer, CNC machine (undergoing refurbishment) plus, a well-equipped electronics lab.

Beginners welcome: Even if you've never used a microcontroller before, come on in! You can use one from the lab and we'll help set you up with some starter projects.

Our Goal is to teach you the skills that you need to do your own projects.

BRING: If you have your own laptop, bring it. If you have your own Arduino or other microcontroller to work on, bring it too.

If you do not have a laptop or a microcontroller, that’s OK! Just please state so in your RSVP so We can be sure to have one available for you from our lab.

JOIN: For information about becoming a member of CDL see http://compdecon.org/membership/

COST: Microcontroller Mondays are Free to CDL members; for non-members there is a $30 fee ($30/month or $300/year). First time visitors to CDL are free.

name: Volunteer Day! (Partnering w/Food Not Bombs Organization)
description:
**We** (our company ***Plant Based Planet LLC***) are excited to be partnering with **Food Not Bombs** in efforts to provide healthy plant-based foods to anyone in need. When we (our company) receive any tips or donations made from online our [website](www.pb-planet.com) or in person orders; we use that money to help provide free meals for those in need in our community.

**Food Not Bombs** is an international, non-profit organization that recovers and shares free food with the public, without restriction. (I LOVE THIS ABOUT THEM!) The Jersey Shore chapter was founded in 2014 and shares free **VEGAN** food in Asbury Park every Sunday. FNB is dedicated to protesting overwhelming systemic food waste, the segregated lack of access to affordable healthy food, the priorities of a government that spends more on military defense than on programs of social uplift, and the overall fragmentation of communities.
[https://www.facebook.com/JerseyShoreFoodNotBombs/
Email: JerseyShoreFoodNotBombs@yahoo.com
Contact: 732-616-8855

**What to expect when arriving to volunteer:**
Assist with set up (Tables, sternos, and positioning of all foods etc)
Serve the public (Handing out produce, groceries, or serving hot meals etc)
Assist with clean up (Breaking down tables, sternos, and cleaning up etc)

**Can't make it to volunteer but want to donate food or money?** Private message me to arrange for any food, household items, clothing, or toiletries to pick up or payment. (You could also contact FNB directly to make arrangements and donations too.) https://account.venmo.com/u/PBFNB[Donate Money](https://account.venmo.com/u/PBFNB)

Join us in our effort to create non-oppressive, **sustainable** change through the empowerment of the people. What I love about this organization is that everything is shared for free regardless of need. **No application, no paperwork, and no ID are necessary.** We need more of this way of helping folks in our world and less firing hoops just to get the help one may need. "Because food is a right, not a privilege."

name: Toastmasters Meeting - to improve Public Speaking & Leadership Skills
description:
Shore Speakers In-Person Venue will be our Original Location.
**Neptune Township Public Library**
**2nd Floor Conference Room**
**25 Neptune Boulevard**
**Neptune, NJ 07753**
**(Between the High School and the Municipal Building)**
**There is Parking and Library Access behind the library building.**

**Note that we always have many more people attending our meetings compare to RSVP on meetup.**
**Meetup is just a way of attracting people of interest to our meetings.**

If you were thinking of attending any of our shore speakers meetings,
do Not miss the opportunity to find out about Toastmasters.
We highly recommend attending in-person.
We now are Only meeting in-person until further notice.
You will feel the positive energy of our in-person meetings,
to improve our public speaking, Communication, and leadership skills in a comfortable atmosphere.
If you have not visited us in a while, visit us to see the excitement of our growing club.
**Meet our new members, and feel the energy of our in-person meetings.**
**See details below.**
**Adults are welcome to any of the**
**Shore Speakers Toastmaster Meetings.**
**Mark your Calendars.**
**We meet on the 2nd & 4th Tuesdays of each month.**

**Our meetings start @ 6:30pm to 7:45pm,**
**Try to show up in-person at 6:00pm.**
**We hope to see you soon.**

name: Insurance For Investment Properties w/ Aaron Levine
description:
Registration starts at 6:30PM.

Learn about the different types of insurance for Fix & Flips, STR, Rentals, Commercial re and more!

About Aaron Levine:

Aaron Levine is the Founder and President of LG Insurance Agency, a boutique, full-service insurance agency located in West Long Branch, New Jersey, that opened in 2009. The Agency has insurance experts to provide local and regional expertise for personal, business, and various specialty insurance needs. Serving all of your needs from homeowners, commercial auto, vacant land and pet insurance too. Aaron Levine has a Bachelor of Science degree in Marketing Management from Syracuse University where he also was active in the Sigma Alpha Epsilon Fraternity and graduated in 2002. In 2008, Aaron graduated from Monmouth University with a Master’s in Business Administration.
Aaron is an avid Triathlete and Marathoner having completed Iron Man in 2008 at the Ford Iron Man in Fla. Community and charity are very important to Aaron which is reflected in the many hats he wears: he serves as the Vice Chairman of FulFill Foodbank of Monmouth and Ocean Counties, the Secretary of PIA (Professional Insurance Agents) of New Jersey, as well the past President of the Greater Long Branch Chamber of Commerce. Giving back to the community is at the center of who Aaron Levine is, but his favorite accomplishment is being a proud girl dad to his two daughters Elle and Daire.

• What we'll do
Real Estate networking with investors, realtors, lenders, attorneys, accountants, and everything else you need to build your real estate powerteam!

• What to bring
Bring your questions, business cards and a good attitude

name: BELMAR BAY KAYAK and Swim!
description:
Come on out and join me on the Bay in Belmar! I will be starting at L Street Bathing Beach and kayaking along the marina after the first bridge, taking a nice dip in the water and then kayaking back into the right side marina and around the island back into L Street Bathing Beach. (\~2 mile of kayaking)

PARKING IS FREE AT THE MARINA on the side close to the boat launch but can fill up fast!
**KAYAKS, PFDs and some experience required**! Waters can get choppy sometimes with boat traffic and wind. Must be comfortable traversing waves.

Paddle Time Kayaks and EndlessSUPcompany do rentals, but you must inquire and book on your own.

Bring sunscreen, your swimming attire and water for hydration!
**HOPE TO SEE YOU THERE!**

name: Healthy Happy Hour Pot Luck Picnic at the Park
description:
*For this Happy Hour Pot Luck Picnic, let's all bring a healthy dish. I have lots of great, what I call LEAN AND GREEN recipes that my clients LOVE! If you prepare one and bring it, not only will you be discovering a new recipe but you'll be sharing it with the group. Please ask me for a recipe and I can send it along!*
MACLEARIE PARK ROUTE 35 BELMAR

*If you are unable or unwilling to prepare a recipe, you can simple bring veggies or fruit. This is a great opportunity to meet members of this group, share a meal and watch the sunset! (fingers crossed that it won't be a rainy day)*

Since this is outdoors at a picnic table, obviously there won't be facilities to warm up a dish, so bring it ready to serve. If you have any lawn games please bring them along! Please RSVP!

Get creative. Each month a different business will be able to speak for 5 minutes on how we can can help you grow your business. Come grow your net worth with us!!! Bring a friend. cost is $25 to cover buffet breakfast and space. Let's grow together. We ask that everyone reserve and RSVP so we can let Stay Gold know how many to prep for each month. To reserve your space you can text Maria at 732-547-9582 with your full name and company. Pay $25 at the door.

name: Summer Beach Day with Black Philadelphia
description:
Summer Beach Day with Black Philadelphia!

Join Black Philadelphia for a day at the beach! Get your towel, beverages, snacks, and sunscreen as we bring our joy to the Garden State. Thank you to our members Anusha for suggesting and hosting this great event.

Agenda - IMPORTANT

The group will meet at Fifth Avenue at Belmar Beach. You'll grab your badge and then frolic and glow.

**COSTS**:
For beach access, a beach badge/wristband is required for anyone ages 14 and up. **YOU MUST PAY $10.25 (including fees) FOR A DAY BADGE TO ACCESS THE BEACH!** Pick up and sale of beach badges are at the Taylor Pavilion (5th and Ocean Avenue) *Check the photos to see the best ways to buy your badge.*

PARKING: Spaces along the boardwalk cost $2/hr

**Active military members** and **dependents** get on to the beach for FREE when they show Active Military ID.
**Gold Star Family Members:** A Parent, legal guardian of a member of the Armed Forces of the United States who died while on active duty for the United States get on to the beach for FREE.
**ALL Veterans** get on the beach for FREE by showing an ID that identifies them as a veteran

As a reminder, attendees are responsible for their own transportation to Belmar Beach and we encourage carpooling. Please feel free to use the comment section below to arrange travel.

Please feel free to extend the invite. Remember to bring your beach necessities, including a towel, plenty of water, and snacks. Also, prepare to take your trash back with you.

For more Belmar Beach info: **[https://belmar.com/content.php?npid=304&pid=304&menu_id=21](https://belmar.com/content.php?npid=304&pid=304&menu_id=21)**

***By RSVP'ing, you agree to indemnify and hold harmless the Black Philadelphia Group Organizers, Co-Organizers, Asst. Organizers, Event Organizers, and/or Host(s) for any injuries, damages, or liabilities incurred before, during, or after event.***

24 Hours Please

We can't wait to see you! Please don't keep us waiting. If you change your RSVP to "no" less than 24 FULL hours prior to an event, you are a "no show".
